validation_thesis: Run an audience pre-sell for a niche template bundle paired with a benchmark pack to test whether a trusted newsletter audience will pay for a productized asset without any service layer.
key_unknowns: Primary: willingness to pay for a productized template + benchmark offer
Secondary: whether the template, the benchmark pack, or the combined bundle is the strongest wedge
Secondary: whether demand supports a meaningful starting price
motion: Audience pre-sell with completed checkout or pre-order as the primary commitment signal
day_by_day_plan: Day 1: Choose one narrow audience job-to-be-done, define a single paid offer, and publish a simple pre-order page with price and delivery date.
Day 2: Send a segmented newsletter pitch asking for completed checkout or pre-order payment.
Day 3: Follow up with clickers and responders, then categorize objections into price, scope, or credibility.
Day 4: Run a second pass with one changed variable: headline, price, or promise.
Day 5: Collect onboarding inputs from early buyers to confirm repeatable delivery without custom service creep.
Day 6: Review conversion metrics and determine whether demand centers on templates, benchmarks, or the bundle.
Day 7: Make a go, revise, or kill decision based on purchases and objection patterns.
success_criteria: Go: 2 or more completed purchases within 7 days
Go: at least 1 buyer cites the benchmarks/examples as a key reason to buy
Go: 20 percent or better click-to-interest rate from the segmented send
Revise: 1 purchase with multiple high-intent replies indicating a narrower scope or different price
Revise: demand clusters around one component of the bundle rather than the full offer
kill_criteria: Kill: 0 completed purchases after 2 segmented sends
Kill: fewer than 5 qualified buyer replies or conversations after the campaign
Kill: all serious prospects ask for bespoke service rather than the product
Kill: closing buyers requires promising custom work that breaks the product wedge
evidence_to_capture: Recipients reached in the relevant segment
Buyer replies explaining why they bought
Non-buyer objections grouped by price, scope, and credibility
Signals of product demand versus service demand
